warm-potato-salad-with-seafood-argiro.name: Warm Potato Salad With Seafood

ingredients.item: A few branches. fennel and parsley.
2 fresh onions (with their leaves), finely chopped
1 juicy lemon, juice and a little zest
A few branches. fennel and parsley.
2 fresh onions (with their leaves), finely chopped
1 juicy lemon, juice and a little zest

instructions: Clean the potatoes in a frame and leave them in a bowl with water for a while.
Fill a pot with water and, once boiled, immerse the squid and boil for 10 minutes.
Remove them with a perforated spoon and, when slightly cool, cut them into very thin rolls.
In the same pot and in the same water, add the shrimp and boil for exactly 2 minutes. We take them out with a drill spoon and let them cool.
Continue by adding some fennel and parsley branches and potatoes to the pot with the same salt water.
Boil them for less than 15 minutes, not to melt. Drain and keep 1-2 tablespoons of water where the ingredients are boiled.
Clean the shrimp and throw out the shells. Strap their heads to get the juice that we pour into the blender's bowl.
Add the olive oil, lemon juice and water we kept and beat the ingredients until homogenized.
In an open bowl, add the potatoes, squid, shrimp, fresh onion, pepper, a little lemon zest, finely chopped herbs and the finocchio.
Pour the ingredients with the olive oil and mix. We're checking the salt and pepper.
Serve the salad as a main dish, warm or at room temperature, garnishing with lemon slices.

watermelon-and-feta-salad.name: Watermelon And Feta Salad

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Mix red onion and lime juice together in a bowl. Refrigerate until onion turns bright pink, about 30 minutes.
Add watermelon, feta cheese, olives, cilantro, parsley, and olive oil to the onion mixture; mix well. Season with salt and pepper. Add brown sugar if lime juice tastes too sharp.

watermelon-granita.name: Watermelon Granita

ingredients.prep: rind removed, flesh cut into 2-inch pieces

ingredients.item: Ground pequin chile or cayenne pepper

instructions: Purée watermelon, lime juice, sugar, and salt in a blender until smooth. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a 13x9" baking dish; discard solids.

Freeze watermelon mixture until edges begin to set, 30–35 minutes. Using a fork, scrape to break up frozen portions. Freeze, scraping and breaking up every 20–30 minutes, until mixture resembles fluffy shaved ice, 2–4 hours.
To serve, divide granita among bowls and sprinkle with ground chile.
Granita can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and keep frozen. Scrape to fluff just before serving.

watermelon-lemonade-ice-pops.name: Watermelon-lemonade Ice Pops
instructions: Combine all ingredients in processor. Puree until very smooth. Divide mixture among 8 ice pop molds (each about 1/4 to 1/3 cup capacity). Cover and freeze until firm, at least 4 hours and up to 5 days.

weekday-meal-prep-turkey-taco-bowls.name: Weekday Meal-prep Turkey Taco Bowls
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the peppers and onions and season with salt and pepper, to taste. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables have softened. Remove from pan, set aside.
Add ground turkey to hot skillet. Break apart with a wooden spoon to separate.
Season with taco seasoning, stir, cooking until meat has browned and cooked through.
Stir in tomatoes. Bring to a gentle simmer for about 5 minutes. Remove from heat.

To assemble the taco bowls, add a base of rice to 4 food storage containers. Top each with a scoop of black beans, corn, salsa, cheddar cheese, cooked peppers and onions, and taco meat. Garnish with fresh cilantro and a lime wedge.
Store in the refrigerator (and enjoy any extras immediately). Can be kept refrigerated for up to 4 days.
Enjoy!

wellington-style-tenderloin-lezzet.name: Wellington Style Tenderloin
instructions: 1. Set oven to 180 degrees.
2. Before cooking the Bonfile, open small cuts from 4-5 to 4-5 pieces with the blade and place the shells peeled garlic to their interior.
3. Mix olive oil, honey, soy sauce, thyme, salt and pepper in a bowl.
4. Remove all the sosu bonfile you prepared.
5. Heat a cast pan thoroughly and remove the bond in high fire, let each side change color (this process makes the bonfile not lose water at the next prying stage, and it is in caramelize flavor).
6. Wait for taking the Bonfile into a tab.
7. Wrap the straws with roller on the lightly unified floor and close each side of the inceltin and bonfile.
8. Insert the additional place to the oiled paper serrated oven tray to the bottom.
9. Paste the figures with increased dough parts on it.
10. Remove the egg yolk and cook about 40 minutes in the oven heated in advance.
11. 11. Serve and serve.

what-is-carre-and-how-to-cook-it-pequere.name: What Is Carre (and How To Cook It)
what-is-carre-and-how-to-cook-it-pequere.original_name: Qué es el CARRÉ (y cómo cocinarlo)

ingredients.item: 4 carries of lamb with 4-5 ribs each

instructions: In a bowl we mix well the oil, vinegar, wine, chopped garlic teeth and thyme and rosemary branches. We add the carries inside, we turn them so that they get well drenched from the marinado we cover the bowl with transparent film and leave at least 4 hours in the refrigerator.

At the time of cooking the meat, we heated the oven at 200 ″ 3 ″. We put the carrot with its marinado on an oven plate, we sprinkle with salt in scales and freshly ground black pepper we bake 15 minutes for the meat to be at its point. If you care about more baked some more minutes, if you care about it less about some less minutes.

white-rice.name: White Rice
instructions: Rinse the rice in a sieve three times, or until the water runs clear.
Add the water and salt to a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Add the rice and stir, letting the water come to a boil again.
Place the lid on the saucepan and lower the heat to a simmer. Simmer for 18 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the rice steam for 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice with a fork.
Enjoy!

white-winebraised-chicken.name: White Wine–braised Chicken

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Heat oil in a Dutch oven or other heavy-lidded pot over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper and cook until golden brown, about 5 minutes per side; transfer to a plate.

Add shallots and garlic to pot and cook, stirring often, until beginning to soften, about 2 minutes. Add thyme and white wine; bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer until reduced by ⅔, about 4 minutes.

Return chicken, skin side up, to pot; add broth, bring to a simmer, cover, and transfer to oven. Braise until chicken is cooked through and tender, 20–25 minutes. Uncover; continue to cook in oven until skin begins to crisp, 8–10 minutes longer.
